title: Safely Reduce the Local OpenCode Session Database
test_framework: PowerShell safety harness plus node:sqlite integration fixtures
pipeline_rationale: This track creates safety-critical automation and performs retention/deletion, backup, SQLite compaction, and reversible replacement of shared user storage. High data-loss and privacy impact require full independent review, RED tests on disposable fixtures, execution, test running, and validation.

skipped_stage_candidates.reason: Skip only if the Stage 2 B+C threshold is not triggered; never skip merely to shorten a high-risk storage track.
Skip only if the Stage 7 A+C threshold is not triggered and no deliverable, retention, rollback, or reporting mismatch remains.
A no-product-doc edit may be waived only with a dated execution-log reason; handover, savings, and rollback records remain mandatory deliverables.

progress.phase: Stage 7 validation complete - at no-candidate gate. Safety harness created and tested. Mutation path cancelled (0 candidates).
progress.note: 8/14 completed (Phase 0 + script creation for 2.1/3.1/3.2 + F.3 reconciliation). 4 cancelled (2.2/3.3/F.1/F.2 - no candidates). 1 deferred (1.2 - approval gate, 0 candidates). 1 in-progress (1.1 - backup script created, not run).

summary: Inventory metadata safely, protect valuable session families, obtain approval for an immutable candidate manifest, delete only through the supported OpenCode CLI, compact reversibly, and report exact disk savings.
keyOutcomes: No message content or credentials appear in inventory or reports
Only explicitly approved sessions are deleted through opencode session delete
Fresh backup and pre-compaction rollback copies remain available
risks: SQLite inconsistency from concurrent writers or WAL mishandling
Private content leakage through inventory or logs
dependencies: Canonical opencode CLI with session delete support
Node runtime with node:sqlite (Node 22.5+ with --experimental-sqlite, or Node 24+ built-in) for backup and query support
Sufficient disk capacity for backup and compact candidate
Stage 4 will scaffold tests\run-tests.ps1 RED-test harness (per test_command field) before Stage 5 execution
blocking: Explicit user approval of generated candidate manifest hash before deletion
deliverables: Execution-time redacted inventory, backup validation, approval, deletion, integrity, compaction, savings, rollback, and handover artifacts
validationCriteria: Manifest hash used for deletion exactly matches explicit approval
quick_check is ok and schema fingerprint/user_version are unchanged
Protected sessions remain and approved sessions are absent
Exact pre/post DB+WAL+SHM byte arithmetic is internally consistent
Application session-list smoke read exits zero

tool_preflight.fallback: PowerShell-first through bounded bash tool calls
tool_preflight.pathRules: Use -LiteralPath and quoted absolute Windows paths; do not retry native file tools in this session

blockers: Manifest has 0 candidates - all 2,776 sessions protected under 180-day policy
OpenCode writers active (7 processes) - backup requires writers stopped
node:sqlite lacks backup() API - sqlite3 CLI not available

test_results.note: All 44 tests pass after creating fail-closed utility scripts and fixing raw JSON console output

execution.tasks_cancelled_reason: Manifest contains 0 candidates (all 2,776 sessions protected under 180-day policy with unarchived and family closure protection). No deletion, compaction, swap, or savings measurement applicable.
execution.tasks_deferred_reason: Approval gate not required when manifest has 0 candidates. Re-enter if policy changes.
execution.tasks_in_progress_reason: Backup script created but not executed. Blockers: active OpenCode writers, node:sqlite lacks backup() API, sqlite3 CLI unavailable.
